source_226: TriageTask, ReviewSession, TaskLaunchContext, TaskCommandSettlement are frozen as the review kernel.
source_228: DecisionEpoch refs appear on TriageTask for consequence entry points.
gate_resolution: par_231 owns all core triage and lease mutation. No sibling track may redefine claim, heartbeat, release, or stale-owner recovery.

source_226: Workspace trust and focus rules freeze same-shell laws and trust-derived writability.
gate_resolution: par_232 owns WorkspaceContinuityEvidenceProjection and trust envelopes. par_242 consumes continuity at completion time without co-owning the projection.

source_227: QueueRankPlan, QueueRankSnapshot, QueueRankEntry, and QueueAssignmentSuggestionSnapshot freeze canonical order versus reviewer-fit suggestions.
gate_resolution: par_233 owns canonical queue order and snapshots. Downstream consumers may read or annotate but not rewrite row order.

source_226: Phase 0 duplicate and replay law remains authoritative.
source_227: DuplicateReviewSnapshot freezes Phase 3 duplicate review boundary.
source_228: Decision supersession hooks require stale consequence invalidation.
gate_resolution: par_234 owns DuplicatePairEvidence, DuplicateReviewSnapshot, and DuplicateResolutionDecision. Idempotency and replay remain outside this owner boundary.

source_227: MoreInfoReplyWindowCheckpoint is the sole timer truth.
gate_resolution: par_236 remains deferred until first-wave task and queue outputs exist; no other track may infer timer truth from UI clocks or delivery timestamps.

source_226: TriageTask keeps only refs to current endpoint context, not direct consequence authority.
source_228: DecisionEpoch is the sole consequence fence for endpoint, approval, urgent escalation, and downstream seeds.
gate_resolution: par_238 owns DecisionEpoch mutation and supersession execution once first-wave evidence kernels exist.

source_228: ApprovalCheckpoint invalidates on endpoint drift, payload drift, patient reply, duplicate resolution, publication drift, trust drift, and epoch supersession.
gate_resolution: par_239 consumes live DecisionEpoch and duplicate invalidation outputs; approval logic may not launch ahead of par_238.

source_228: Booking and pharmacy intents are peer consequence domains, not status flags.
source_229: Callback, clinician-message, self-care, and admin remain peer governed domains with separate leases and settlements.
gate_resolution: par_240 creates peer continuation seeds; par_243, par_244, par_249, and par_251 later implement their own domain kernels.

source_229: Contact repair is a governed seam across callback, message, and patient conversation surfaces.
gate_resolution: par_245 owns governed repair and controlled resend logic. Communication domains may surface repair state but may not silently retry around it.

source_226: LifecycleCoordinator remains the only request-closure authority.
source_229: Admin and callback completion remain domain-local until canonical closure rules run.
gate_resolution: No Phase 3 track may close a request directly. Completion and consequence tracks settle local domains only.

source_226: First wave is intended to open from seq_230 gate.
source_229: Shared operating contract explicitly opens 231 to 235 in parallel.
gate_resolution: Launch readiness ignores the sibling consume-order prompt drift and instead follows the seq_230 dependency interface map and launch packets.
Tracks 252 to 254 remain blocked because their prompt files are empty and therefore lack executable ownership, tests, and fail-closed conditions.
